name: Italian Game
description: A classical e4 opening where White develops the bishop to c4, eyeing f7 — Black's weakest square. The Italian leads to sharp tactical play and timeless attacks.

moves.instruction: Stake a claim in the center and open lines for the bishop and queen.
Black claims central space in kind, freeing the kingside pieces.
Develop with a threat — the knight attacks e5.
Defend e5 with the knight, completing a natural developing move.
The Italian move. The bishop aims at f7, Black's most fragile point.
Mirror White — Giuoco Piano ("quiet game"). Black targets f2 in return.
Build a pawn chain to support a future d4 advance in the center.
Develop and add a second attacker to e4, creating tension.
Open the center now that Black's pieces are committed.
Capture toward the center, opening the e-file for the Black rook later.
Recapture, giving White a strong central pawn majority.
Check to disrupt White — pinning the c3-knight against the king.
Block and develop. White accepts doubled pawns in exchange for the bishop pair.
Grab the free pawn — this is the critical test of the line.
Castle, sacrificing the pawn back for a lead in development and the open center.
Trade and give White doubled c-pawns — White gets the bishop pair in return.
